structure.description: Keyboard-triggered overlay (default Cmd+O) with fuzzy-match input over entity titles. Up/down to navigate candidates, Enter to switch active tab to the selected entity. Distinct from inline-search: the goal is navigation (replace current focus), not result-listing. Distinct from global-command-palette: scope is a single entity-type, not commands.

rationale.hypothesis: Switching between named entities in a flat namespace (1000+ notes, files, contacts) is the highest-frequency navigation in editor-class tools. Mouse-driven navigation through tree/sidebar doesn't scale past ~50 items. Fuzzy quick-switcher reduces navigation latency to keyboard-only, exploits typed-name-as-best-handle, and is composable with workspace tabs (replace tab.entity rather than open new window).

evidence.description: Cmd+O quick switcher: fuzzy file-by-name → switch tab
Cmd+P quick-open: same UX over file titles
Cmd+K with project-scoped fuzzy issue switcher
T (file finder) in repo views — keyboard fuzzy-jump

counterexample.description: No quick switcher; scale of one document at a time — pattern doesn't apply

shouldMatch.reason: Cmd+P over Page.title is canonical Notion UX

shouldNotMatch.reason: Courier sees 1-3 active orders; fuzzy switcher is over-engineered
Bookings are time-driven; fuzzy-by-title doesn't fit the workflow
